title: selinux vs. podman play kube volume
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20756
Originally posted by **bfallik** November 22, 2023 Hi, I'm trying to migrate an existing manual container deployment to `podman play kube` but I can't seem to setup the volume with correct selinux bits. I have a USB disk that's mounted under `/mnt`. When using podman to create the container manually, I mounted the volume like so: ``` ... -v "/mnt/One Touch/backups/repo:/data/repo:Z" \ ``` However, now that I'm using `podman kube play`, I can't figure out how to pass that 'Z'. I've tried this: ``` apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: server annotations: bind-mount-options: "/mnt/One Touch/backups/repo:Z" ``` but the container just hangs in the `Created` state: ``` $ podman ps -a CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES f2e2ec9c5460 localhost/podman-pause:4.7.2-1698762721 6 minutes ago Up 6 minutes 09d3badcb7f9-service dd08057b1c77 localhost/podman-pause:4.7.2-1698762721 6 minutes ago Up 6 minutes 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp 14d376a74a5d-infra 6d846bf325a3 docker.io/restic/rest-server:0.12.1 /entrypoint.sh 6 minutes ago Created 0.0.0.0:8000->8000/tcp rest-server-rest-server ``` Any idea what's going on here? Let me know if I can provide more details. Thx!

comments.body: Same issue here. I'm in the process of creating a kube yaml for Netbox and am stuck at the point of passing SELinux labels `:z` or `:Z`.
